page.title: Projects
page.summary: Selected work at the intersection of computer vision, geometry, and machine learning. Each project links a concrete problem to the tools I reached for.
items.vehicles.title: Visual analysis of moving vehicles
items.vehicles.summary: A geometric framework for vehicle motion analysis and collision detection, built on 3D reconstruction and projective geometry.
items.vehicles.body: Existing collision-detection pipelines lean on motion features or deep networks. This project takes a different route: given known camera intrinsics and standard vehicle dimensions, headlights become reliable reference points. From those, projective geometry yields vanishing points, vehicle orientation, and — through back-projection and the law of cosines — real-world inter-vehicle distance.
items.survival.title: Cardiac calcification and survival in lung-cancer radiotherapy
items.survival.summary: Extracted calcified regions from CT scans via image segmentation, then evaluated their effect on patient survival with Cox proportional-hazards analysis.
items.survival.body: Automated Agatston-style cardiac calcification scoring from CT DICOM images in Python, paired with survival analysis in R. The pipeline turns imaging biomarkers into statistically testable predictors of overall survival for lung-cancer patients undergoing radiotherapy.
items.mars.title: Segmentation of Mars terrain
items.mars.summary: A deep-learning model for pixel-level classification of Mars terrain, tuned for high mean intersection-over-union.
items.mars.body: Trained and evaluated a segmentation architecture on Mars orbital imagery, achieving high MIoU across terrain classes. Emphasis on class balance, augmentation strategy, and boundary-aware loss.
items.self-supervise.title: Self Supervised Denoising
items.self-supervise.summary: Implementation and comparison of three self supervised denoising methods.
items.self-supervise.body: Self Supervised algorithms don't require clean training data. Three self-supervised and unsupervised algorithms (Deep Image Prior (DIP), Self2Self, Neighbor2Neighbor) were implemented in tensorflow and results were compared.
